mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 3.5% signals a tightening labor market, which increases corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and GDP signals strong supply-side capacity utilization, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO/GDPC1 growth and SPX returns falls below 0.15 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts above 1.3 million units signals strong consumer demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during post-recession recoveries.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 50%+ of instances where HOUST exceeds 1.3M during recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in the federal funds rate above 5% signals monetary tightening, which increases equity discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls and declining unemployment signals strong labor market dynamics, which drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS/UNRATE on SPX returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) and rising federal funds rates signal a monetary tightening cycle, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 50%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in retail sales (RSAFS) above 720 billion dollars signals strong consumer demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 50%+ of instances where RSAFS exceeds 720B.
